en.extract:
The buff-bellied monarch (Neolalage banksiana) is a species of bird in the family Monarchidae. It is monotypic within the genus Neolalage. It is endemic to Vanuatu, where its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.
The buff-bellied monarch was originally described in the genus Lalage. Alternate names include Banksian monarch, buff-bellied flycatcher, New Hebrides flycatcher, New Hebrides monarch, Pacific monarch (a name shared with the pale-blue monarch) and Vanuatu flycatcher.

The '''buff-bellied monarch''' ('''''Neolalage banksiana''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Monarchidae]]. It is [[monotypic]] within the genus '''''Neolalage'''''.<ref name= "ITIS"/> It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Vanuatu]], where its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

The buff-bellied monarch was originally described in the genus ''[[Triller|Lalage]]''. Alternate names include '''Banksian monarch''', '''buff-bellied flycatcher''', '''New Hebrides flycatcher''', '''New Hebrides monarch''', '''Pacific monarch''' (a name shared with the [[pale-blue monarch]]) and '''Vanuatu flycatcher'''.
